notes: SHARED-DNS / SHARED-RESOLVER TRAP. A Corefile change (commit 1f3d5b79, deployed 09:00) shrank the CoreDNS in-memory cache to 1000 entries and pointed the forward plugin at a slow internet upstream with max_concurrent 100; on reload the resolver's cache fills and evicts constantly while forwarded lookups queue behind the saturated upstream, so SERVFAIL rate, cache evictions, and forward p99 latency on coredns-cluster all climb FIRST. Three UNRELATED services that each resolve external hostnames per-request through the cluster DNS -- web-checkout (resolves the payment gateway host), email-worker (resolves the SMTP relay host), sync-agent (resolves the object-store/S3 endpoint host) -- all start returning getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND / resolution timeouts at the same moment. They share NO service-call edge in service_map.json: they sit in different call graphs and only co-depend on dns:coredns-cluster. web-checkout is LOUDEST/paged (checkout failures). Control services metrics-relay (static hostAliases IP, no DNS) and image-proxy (NodeLocal DNS cache + long-lived cached connection) do NOT depend on cluster DNS at request time and stay healthy, proving the fault is the shared resolver, not a global network outage. Origin is the CoreDNS resolver saturation after the Corefile change.
_design_note: Paged on web-checkout because checkout failures are the most revenue-visible symptom. web-checkout is one of three services whose outbound DNS lookups fail simultaneously; check WHY (the shared cluster DNS resolver SERVFAIL/eviction/forward-latency spike), not just web-checkout's own logs. pdf-renderer errored AND just deployed but is an off-resolver decoy.
